characters.name: Carl
characters.description: The titular protagonist. A former coast‑guardsman turned crawler, Carl and his ex‑girlfriend’s cat (Donut) become fan‑favorite contestants on the Syndicate gameshow Dungeon Crawler World:contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}.

characters.name: Princess Donut
characters.description: A tortoiseshell Persian cat and the deuteragonist.  She has the highest total stat points in the party, so she becomes party leader and renames their group the "Royal Court of Princess Donut."  She acquires the Enchanted Crown of the Sepsis Whore, which requires her to slay the entire Blood Sultanate royal family:contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}.

characters.name: Mordecai
characters.description: An experienced former crawler who becomes a guide and commentator for Carl and Donut.  He often explains the dungeon mechanics and acts as mentor (description summarized from multiple wiki pages).

characters.name: Mongo
characters.description: A giant spider adopted by Donut near the end of the Second Floor.  Although Mongo’s adoption occurs in Book 2, his presence starts to influence the group in later chapters.

characters.name: Cascadia
characters.description: One of the early crawlers listed in the character master list:contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}.  Specific details are scant, but she is part of the broader cast introduced in Book 1.

characters.name: Cerberus
characters.description: A named character (possibly an NPC or mob) appearing in the character list:contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}.  Detailed background is not provided on the master list.

characters.name: Chaco
characters.description: A character appearing in the early alphabetical portion of the character list:contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}.  He is mentioned without a detailed description.

characters.name: Chin'Dua
characters.description: Another character from the master list:contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}.  Specific narrative role is not given in the category page.

locations_or_places.name: The World Dungeon
locations_or_places.description: An eighteen‑level maze created beneath Earth's surface after the Transformation.  It appears in Book 1 Chapter 2 and serves as the primary setting for the gameshow Dungeon Crawler World:contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}.

locations_or_places.name: First Floor
locations_or_places.description: The tutorial floor accessed from surface‑level staircases after the Transformation.  It is a sprawling grid of passages and tunnels hewn from rock, split into unconnected sections so crawlers from different regions cannot interact:contentReference[oaicite:7]{index=7}.

locations_or_places.name: Dungeon Entrance
locations_or_places.description: The stairway/ramp that appears in streets across Earth during the Transformation; Agatha is the first to go down voluntarily, demonstrating that the stairs can transform into a ramp for accessibility:contentReference[oaicite:8]{index=8}.

locations_or_places.name: Desperado Club
locations_or_places.description: One of the dungeon’s notable locations; it appears in the trending page list of dungeon locations:contentReference[oaicite:9]{index=9}.

locations_or_places.name: Larracos
locations_or_places.description: A location within the dungeon, listed among trending dungeon locations:contentReference[oaicite:10]{index=10}.

locations_or_places.name: Club Vanquisher
locations_or_places.description: Another location in the dungeon, part of the trending locations list:contentReference[oaicite:11]{index=11}.

locations_or_places.name: Grimaldi’s Traveling Circus
locations_or_places.description: A circus within the dungeon environment that becomes significant in later floors; it is mentioned in the trending locations list:contentReference[oaicite:12]{index=12}.

locations_or_places.name: Zockau
locations_or_places.description: A dungeon location included in the trending list:contentReference[oaicite:13]{index=13}.

locations_or_places.name: Abyss Station
locations_or_places.description: Another notable dungeon location from the trending list:contentReference[oaicite:14]{index=14}.

locations_or_places.name: Personal Space
locations_or_places.description: A unique location inside the dungeon where crawlers can rest or regroup; it appears on the trending location list:contentReference[oaicite:15]{index=15}.

timeline_events.event: Transformation and emergence of the World Dungeon
timeline_events.description: A global event called the Transformation causes massive stairways to appear across Earth, leading to the newly‑created World Dungeon.  The dungeon becomes the setting for the syndicated gameshow Dungeon Crawler World:contentReference[oaicite:16]{index=16}.

timeline_events.event: Carl and Donut enter the dungeon
timeline_events.description: Following the Transformation, Carl and his ex‑girlfriend’s cat Donut descend into the World Dungeon, beginning their careers as crawlers on the gameshow (synthesized from book synopsis).

timeline_events.event: Skull Empire referenced on Death Watch Extreme Dungeon Mayhem
timeline_events.description: During a broadcast of the in‑universe show Death Watch Extreme Dungeon Mayhem, Maestro claims the Skull Clan existed when the Syndicate created the crawl.  Zev notes that the Skull Empire is one of the largest and oldest Syndicate governments:contentReference[oaicite:17]{index=17}.

factions.name: Blood Sultanate
factions.description: A faction noted on the factions list.  It is a royal family targeted by Donut’s crown quest; the crown requires her to slay the entire Blood Sultanate royal family:contentReference[oaicite:19]{index=19}.

factions.name: Bugbear
factions.description: A faction mentioned on the factions page’s list:contentReference[oaicite:20]{index=20}.  Specific description is not provided in the category, but Bugbears are likely a species or group encountered in the dungeon.

factions.name: Burrowers
factions.description: Another faction listed among the factions.  Details are sparse in the category page, but they are one of the groups active within the dungeon world:contentReference[oaicite:21]{index=21}.

factions.name: Dreadnoughts
factions.description: One of the factions listed on the factions page:contentReference[oaicite:22]{index=22}.  The Dreadnoughts are likely a military‑style group or team competing in Faction Wars.

factions.name: Dream
factions.description: A faction appearing on the factions page:contentReference[oaicite:23]{index=23}.  Further details are not provided in the category summary.

factions.name: Lemig Sortion
factions.description: A lesser‑known faction from the factions list:contentReference[oaicite:24]{index=24}.

factions.name: Naga
factions.description: A faction or race listed among the factions:contentReference[oaicite:25]{index=25}.  The Nagas may represent a serpentine race within the dungeon.

factions.name: Princess Posse
factions.description: A faction formed by Carl and Donut during the Ninth Floor Faction Wars.  It is sponsored by the Society for the Eradication of Cocker Spaniels and is co‑led by Carl and Princess Donut:contentReference[oaicite:26]{index=26}.

factions.name: Prism Kingdom
factions.description: A faction included on the factions list:contentReference[oaicite:27]{index=27}.  Additional details are not given in the category.

factions.name: Skull Empire
factions.description: A monarchy run by King Rust.  In Book 1, Maestro claims the Skull Clan existed when the Syndicate created the crawl, and Zev compares the Skull Empire to a mega‑corporation, noting it is one of the largest and oldest Syndicate governments:contentReference[oaicite:29]{index=29}.

factions.name: Viceroys
factions.description: A Syndicate race and government known for necromancy and horrific battlefield spells.  They regularly compete in the Ninth Floor Faction Wars as “The Madness”:contentReference[oaicite:30]{index=30}.
